title: Worcester man receives life sentence for home invasion
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Worcester man Concepcion Rodriguez sentenced to life for 2022 Fitchburg home invasion, with additional charges, says DA.
Rodriguez found guilty of multiple charges including armed assault, firearm possession, and theft; one firearm charge dismissed.
Rodriguez's attorney claims wrongful conviction and intends to appeal based on habitual criminal penalties and Eighth Amendment rights.

title: Derrick White talks beer, being a Bostonian, and a promising Celtics playoff run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Derrick White and other Boston sports stars helped launch Samuel Adams’ new beer line at an event on Saturday.
White emphasized his connection with Boston, both on and off the court, as he embraces being part of the Celtics community.
The Celtics, overcoming initial doubt, are positioned as strong contenders for the NBA title with a 53-25 record.

title: Mike Krzyzewski threw a subtle jab at Bill Belichick in response to new UNC hire
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: UNC hired former Nuggets coach Michael Malone as their men's basketball coach, replacing Hubert Davis.
Mike Krzyzewski commented on Malone's hire, noting the challenge of transitioning from pro to college level coaching.
UNC's head football and basketball programs are led by former pro coaches, Bill Belichick and Michael Malone respectively.

title: Person stabs dog in Milton during alleged attack
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A person in Milton allegedly stabbed a dog that was attacking them, according to State Police.
The incident occurred Monday morning on Blue Hill River Road near a park and recreation area.
State Police report the dog was taken to a local vet, and Milton Animal Control is investigating.

title: Alex Cora reshuffles Red Sox lineup, drops Trevor Story out of No. 2 spot
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Trevor Story moved to No. 5 in Red Sox lineup after starting season with a .119 average.
Designated hitter Masataka Yoshida now bats second, while Roman Anthony leads off for Boston.
Red Sox seek improvement with lineup changes amid a 2-7 season start, facing Brewers' Brandon Woodruff.

title: Cleveland radio host says Cavaliers are ‘scared’ of facing the Celtics in the playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cleveland Cavaliers prefer the No. 4 seed to avoid facing the Celtics in the second playoff round.
Cavs have failed to advance past the second playoff round since 2018, with recent losses to the Celtics.
Cavaliers' players, including Mobley and Allen, missed recent games due to injuries, affecting their lineup choices.

title: Where you live in Mass. can shape your electric bill — is yours getting out of control?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts residents have the highest electricity costs in the continental U.S., varying greatly by locality.
Customers in 52 Mass. communities pay lower electricity rates due to locally-owned municipal utilities.
Switching from investor-owned to municipal utilities is costly, requiring voter approval and infrastructure purchase.

title: Northeastern students rally over lack of communication after recent violence
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Northeastern students rally for better communication after violent incidents near campus, citing lack of transparency from administration.
A recent fatal police shooting on Hemenway Street and a series of stabbings have heightened safety concerns among students.
Northeastern vice president stated incidents occurred off-campus; increased patrols and improved communication have been promised.

title: BU president pauses signage policy after Pride flag controversy
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: BU President Melissa Gilliam pauses signage policy amid Pride flag removal backlash on campus.
Controversy erupted after Pride flags were removed in March, sparking protests and a petition led by students and faculty.
The policy pause aims to foster dialogue and reflection on university values and community respect.

title: Red Sox complete Sonny Gray trade, send pitching prospect to Cardinals
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Red Sox finalized the Sonny Gray trade by sending pitcher Patrick Galle to the St. Louis Cardinals.
Gray, acquired with cash in November, debuted for the Red Sox with a 6-5 loss against the Reds.
Patrick Galle, a 22-year-old right-handed relief pitcher, was drafted by Boston from Ole Miss in 2025.
